key_insight: The paper reframes continuous high-order finite elements so the cell-wise (element-local) vector becomes the persistent primary data structure, enabling matrix-free GPU solves without assembling global vectors except where the preconditioner requires it.
engineering_relevance: This can simplify and accelerate high-order FEM on GPUs by improving data locality and reducing indirect communication, which is valuable for large-scale solid mechanics and other PDE simulations.
method: Uses a primal-dual pairing identity to show Krylov scalars match the assembled solve on the unassembled representation.
Implements direct stiffness summation via dimensionally split face-exchange sweeps, avoiding indirect gather-scatter, atomics, and coloring.
Handles unstructured macro-block interfaces and h-adaptive hanging nodes with separate topological kernels and a shadow-cell wrapper.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on modern GPUs showing the operator evaluation and solver outperform state-of-the-art matrix-free implementations, with throughput significantly exceeding existing methods.
watch_item: Performance depends on the preconditioner and on how well the blocked cell-wise layout matches the mesh/topology, so benefits may vary across problem types and GPU architectures.
paper_title: Coalesced Matrix-Free Finite Elements in Cell-Wise Storage
